assertion_results.text: Step 7 detects concurrent triage TC-8019 on the same upstream component (quinn-proto) with status In Progress
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 23-31: JQL search returned 1 result showing 'TC-8019 | In Progress | engineer-b@example.com'. Line 31 states: 'Concurrent triage **detected**. Another engineer (`engineer-b@example.com`) is actively triaging TC-8019, which affects the same upstream component (`quinn-proto`). TC-8019 is currently `In Progress`'.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage warning includes the conflicting CVE issue key (TC-8019) and its assignee
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 38-53 present a warning block that includes a table with 'TC-8019 | In Progress | engineer-b@example.com', explicitly showing both the conflicting issue key (TC-8019) and its assignee (engineer-b@example.com).

assertion_results.text: The output presents three options: wait for the other triage to complete, skip remediation task creation, or proceed with a concurrent-triage-overlap label
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 48-53 list three options: '1. Wait -- pause until the other triage completes, then re-run Step 4.3 to detect any overlap', '2. Skip -- skip remediation task creation for this CVE', '3. Proceed -- create tasks anyway with a `concurrent-triage-overlap` label so the other engineer’s Step 4.3 catches the overlap'.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage check runs before Case A/B/C branching in Step 8
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md line 65: 'The engineer must choose one of the three options before the triage can continue to Case A/B/C branching in Step 8'. Also triage-outcome.md lines 62-64: 'Remediation task creation is currently blocked by Step 7 (Concurrent Triage Detection).' followed by Case A/B/C classification in the Triage Decision section, confirming Step 7 gates Step 8.
